strengths: ~20 years of continuous software engineering experience spanning embedded systems, high-throughput data infrastructure, web/mobile, and developer tooling — comfortably clears the 15-year minimum.
Genuine entrepreneurial track record: founded and sustains GISCollective as an open-source geo-data platform serving real conservation NGOs, demonstrating product ownership and long-term commitment.
Recent hands-on exposure to agentic AI infrastructure at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows — directly relevant to the AI focus of the role.
concerns: AI expertise is recent, narrow, and unrecognized externally: no publications, no AI-centric products at scale, and no visible thought leadership in AI that would support advising strategic customers or inspiring industry peers.

No advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in an AI-related field, and the two bachelor's degrees (Computer Science + Fine Arts/Photography) do not substitute for the depth of ML/AI theory the preferred qualifications emphasize.

No demonstrated experience operating at director-level scope — advising strategic enterprise customers, shaping cloud product roadmaps, or driving cross-organizational AI strategy — which is the core accountability of this principal IC role.
response.justification: The candidate brings broad, multi-decade engineering experience and a genuine builder mentality evidenced by open-source product ownership and diverse technical environments, which satisfy the baseline experience requirement. However, the Principal Engineer (AI Strategy) role is fundamentally an external-facing, director-level advisory position requiring deep and publicly recognized AI expertise — the ability to shape cloud AI product direction, influence strategic customers, and inspire the broader industry. The candidate's AI work is confined to a single recent role at the tooling/infrastructure level, with no track record of shipping AI-powered products at scale, no thought-leadership artifacts (papers, talks, frameworks) in the AI space, and no evidence of operating at the organizational scope this role demands. The gap between breadth-generalist software craftsperson and a recognized AI strategy principal is substantial.
